_doc: Stale-terminology lint rules — patterns flagged across user-visible surfaces (i18n bundles + index.html + every _partials/**/*.html).
Each entry: { pattern: <regex string>, replacement_hint: <human suggestion>, reason: <why this drift class matters>, allowlist: <optional list of file paths where the pattern is intentional> }.
Update this list whenever a section / tab / label gets renamed: add the OLD literal here so any leftover site fails the lint at CI time.
Run: python tests/test_no_stale_section_names.py — exits non-zero on any unallowlisted hit.

rules.reason: The Host stats settings tab was renamed to Admin → Providers. Old breadcrumb is a dead navigation pointer.
Same rename — operator-visible references should say Providers.
Modal / form titles that still call the section by its old name.
Bare 'Host stats' inside any double-quoted i18n value or attribute is a renamed-tab leak. The double-quote anchor avoids matching unrelated phrases inside paragraphs.
